pattern: Greedy set-maximization: contribute unique elements from each array, prioritizing elements exclusive to one array, and cap each array's contribution at n/2.
hints: Compute the sets of unique values in nums1 and nums2, and their intersection; each array must contribute exactly n/2 elements to the union.
Elements unique to nums1 contribute to the set for free (they don't collide with nums2); same for elements unique to nums2.
The final set size is: min(|unique1|, n/2) + min(|unique2|, n/2) + shared elements that fill remaining slots.

edge_inputs.description: All elements identical in both arrays
Completely disjoint — maximum possible set size n
One array has fewer unique values than n/2 — shared values must fill the gap

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = size
    # half shared, half disjoint
    shared = [rng.randint(1, n) for _ in range(n // 2)]
    unique1 = [rng.randint(n+1, 2*n) for _ in range(n // 2)]
    unique2 = [rng.randint(2*n+1, 3*n) for _ in range(n // 2)]
    nums1 = (shared + unique1)
    nums2 = (shared + unique2)
    rng.shuffle(nums1)
    rng.shuffle(nums2)
    return [nums1, nums2]

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def maximumSetSize(self, nums1, nums2):
        n = len(nums1)
        half = n // 2
        s1 = set(nums1)
        s2 = set(nums2)
        common = s1 & s2
        only1 = s1 - s2
        only2 = s2 - s1
        # Greedy: maximize distinct values
        # From nums1, take min(|only1|, half) unique-to-1 values
        # From nums2, take min(|only2|, half) unique-to-2 values
        # Fill remaining slots from shared
        take1_unique = min(len(only1), half)
        take2_unique = min(len(only2), half)
        # remaining capacity in each half
        rem1 = half - take1_unique
        rem2 = half - take2_unique
        # shared fills both remaining slots
        from_shared = min(len(common), rem1 + rem2)
        return take1_unique + take2_unique + from_shared

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def maximumSetSize(self, nums1, nums2):
        n = len(nums1)
        half = n // 2
        s1 = set(nums1)
        s2 = set(nums2)
        only1 = s1 - s2
        only2 = s2 - s1
        common = s1 & s2
        take1 = min(len(only1), half)
        take2 = min(len(only2), half)
        rem1 = half - take1
        rem2 = half - take2
        shared = min(len(common), rem1 + rem2)
        return take1 + take2 + shared
